exampleSentencesTranslated: He forgot to bring an umbrella, therefore he got wet when it rained.
She has a sore throat, therefore she cannot come to class today.
I finished my homework, therefore I can rest.

translation.en: I have finished studying, therefore I will go to sleep.

content.sentence: Tôi đã học xong, ___ tôi sẽ đi ngủ.

notes:
vì vậy means therefore or so — it shows a result from something said before.

It connects two clauses: reason → result. The first clause gives the cause, and vì vậy introduces the consequence.

It’s a bit more formal and clear than nên (also “so”), and similar to do đó (more formal). Use it when you want to sound logical and structured.
notes_md:
**vì vậy** means *therefore* or *so* — it shows a result from something said before.

It connects two clauses: reason → result. The first clause gives the cause, and **vì vậy** introduces the consequence.


It’s a bit more formal and clear than **nên** (also “so”), and similar to **do đó** (more formal). Use it when you want to sound logical and structured.
exampleSentences: Anh ấy quên mang ô, vì vậy anh ấy bị ướt khi trời mưa.
Chị bị đau họng, vì vậy chị không thể đến lớp hôm nay.
Em đã hoàn thành xong bài tập, vì vậy em có thể nghỉ ngơi.

example.en: It was raining heavily, so we stayed at home.
example.vi: Trời mưa lớn, vì vậy chúng tôi ở nhà.

definitions.en: A phrase used to indicate the result or consequence of something mentioned earlier.
definitions.vi: Từ dùng để chỉ kết quả hoặc lý do của một sự việc đã nêu trước đó.

translation.en: I have finished studying, therefore I will go to sleep.

content.sentence: Tôi đã học xong, ___ tôi sẽ đi ngủ.
